item.title: UAE Says Iran Cannot Be Trusted Over Hormuz Peace Efforts
item.description: Two months into the conflict, the UAE foreign minister stated that Iran cannot be trusted in negotiations over the Strait of Hormuz blockade. The blockade has choked off 20% of the world oil and gas supplies, pushing up global energy prices.

item.title: Trump Attacks European Leaders Over Iran Response
item.description: US President Donald Trump continued his criticism of German Chancellor Friedrich Merz, saying he was "doing a terrible job" and should spend less time interfering with Washington efforts on Iran. The attacks highlight worsening transatlantic relations.

item.title: Japanese Yen Surges After Suspected Intervention
item.description: The Japanese yen jumped sharply against the dollar on Friday, a day after Tokyo authorities were widely believed to have intervened to prop up the currency. The move highlights growing currency market volatility.

item.title: Syria Relies on Russian Oil Despite Western Pivot
item.description: Russia has emerged as the main supplier of oil to Syria despite the new government alignment with the West, Reuters reporting shows. The situation highlights complex regional energy dynamics amid the broader Middle East conflict.

item.title: Ukraine Seeks Japanese Arms After Export Rules Eased
item.description: Japan relaxation of weapons-export rules opens a path for talks that could lead to Tokyo supplying military equipment to help Ukraine resist Russia invasion, Kyiv ambassador to Japan told Reuters.

item.title: Japan joins US-Philippines military drills in historic first since WWII
item.description: Japanese Ground Self-Defense Force personnel participated in the annual Salaknib exercise with US and Philippine forces, marking the first deployment of combat-ready Japanese troops in the Philippines since World War II amid growing regional deterrence against China.

item.title: Deadly Pakistan-Afghanistan border clashes kill dozens of soldiers
item.description: Pakistan reported 23 soldiers killed while the Taliban claimed 58 Pakistani and 9 Afghan soldiers dead in one of the deadliest border confrontations since the Taliban regained power in 2021, triggered by cross-border militant attacks.

item.title: Pakistani strikes hit Afghan university killing 7, Taliban reports
item.description: Mortars and missiles fired from Pakistan struck a university and civilian homes in northeastern Afghanistan, killing at least 7 and wounding 85, in the first major violence since China-mediated talks between the two sides.

item.title: UAE says Iran cannot be trusted over Hormuz, peace efforts at impasse
item.description: Two months into the US-Iran conflict, the Hormuz blockade has choked off 20% of global oil and gas supplies. UAE warns Iran is not a reliable negotiating partner as ceasefire efforts stall.

item.title: Trump attacks on European leaders worsen transatlantic frost
item.description: President Trump's criticism of European leaders deepens the rift in the transatlantic relationship. Comes amid discussions about reducing US troop presence in Germany and broader NATO tensions.

item.title: Taiwan concerned after China calls island biggest risk in US-China relations
item.description: Taiwan expressed concern after top US and Chinese diplomats held talks where Beijing stressed that the self-ruled island was the biggest risk factor in bilateral relations.

item.title: UAE exit from OPEC may not affect crude prices as anticipated
item.description: Analysis suggests UAE withdrawal from OPEC may have less impact on crude prices than widely expected. UAE became first Gulf state to leave the oil cartel.

item.title: Dubai chefs shrink menus as Iran war disrupts food supply chains
item.description: Restaurants in Dubai are reducing menu offerings as the Iran-US conflict affects shipping routes and makes imported ingredients like avocados and scallops harder to source.

item.title: Japan Intervenes to Prop Up Yen, Spent Estimated $35 Billion
item.description: Japan likely spent around 5.48 trillion yen ($35 billion) to support its currency, BOJ data shows, after reports Tokyo intervened Thursday to arrest a sharp selloff worsened by oil price spikes from the Iran war.

item.title: Protests Erupt in Australian Town Over Indigenous Girl Murder
item.description: Hundreds of protesters clashed with emergency services in Alice Springs after the arrest of a man suspected of murdering a 5-year-old Indigenous girl, prompting authorities to urge calm and tighten alcohol controls.

item.title: Toyota Forecasts Fourth Straight Quarterly Profit Drop as Middle East Costs Mount
item.description: Toyota expects a fourth consecutive year-on-year decline in operating profit as rising material costs, labor expenses, and U.S. tariffs offset strong demand for hybrid vehicles amid Iran war disruptions.

item.title: Lithuania President Backs Joining US Coalition in Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Lithuania President Gitanas Nauseda voiced support for joining the U.S. freedom of navigation mission in the Strait of Hormuz, saying he would present the proposal to the Baltic states defence council.

item.title: Huawei Expects AI Chip Revenue to Jump at Least 60% This Year
item.description: Huawei projects revenue from its AI chips to surge at least 60% in 2026, driven by strong demand from Chinese companies for domestic alternatives amid U.S. export restrictions.

item.title: Oil Hits Four-Year High Above on Iran War Escalation Fears
item.description: Global oil prices eased after hitting a four-year high of more than per barrel on concerns the US-Iran conflict could lead to prolonged Middle East supply disruption with deeper global economic damage.

item.title: Ukrainian Drones Strike Russian Oil Refinery 1,500 km From Frontlines
item.description: Ukraine's SBU security service said its drones struck an oil refinery deep inside Russia near Perm, marking a second consecutive attack on energy facilities as Kyiv seeks to curb Moscow's war funding.

item.title: Germany sheds pacifism but society unprepared for radical military shift
item.description: Berlin has unveiled plans to become the strongest army in Europe, but the country is rearming without redefining its role in the post-war order. The rapid transformation raises questions about German society's readiness for such change.

item.title: Turkey deports dual citizen for serving in Israeli Defense Forces
item.description: A dual citizen of Turkey and Israel was detained in Istanbul after authorities discovered she had served in the Israeli military. The deportation highlights Turkey's firm stance against citizens serving in foreign militaries.

item.title: Lebanese premier vows to reclaim every inch of occupied land
item.description: Prime Minister Nawaf Salam's Labour Day message pledged reforms, job creation and improved living conditions amid economic strain, while vowing to reclaim all occupied Lebanese territory from Israel.

item.title: Iran war drives US gas prices toward summer travel crunch
item.description: The ongoing US-Iran conflict is pushing gas prices higher and disrupting summer travel plans. Airlines are cutting flights and raising ticket prices as jet fuel costs soar, with drivers facing pump prices not seen in years.

item.title: Iran Threatens Painful Strikes if US Renews Attacks as Hormuz Standoff Continues
item.description: Iran warned it would respond with long and painful strikes on US positions if Washington renews attacks, while also restating its claim to the Strait of Hormuz, complicating US efforts to build a coalition to reopen the critical waterway.
